title: Multi-Vendor Fabric Upgrade Compliance — Switch OS / Firmware Waves, Stragglers, EoL Countdown, and Per-Role Posture
exclusions: Cisco-only NDFC Image Management golden lists without Arista, Juniper, SONiC, or Cumulus counterparts (see UC-18.4.10 — single-vendor NDFC posture, not cross-vendor wave logic); SDN controller quorum or HA narratives lacking device NOS version artifacts (see UC-18.3.3); VXLAN or EVPN overlay fault telemetry lacking upgrade-wave intent (see UC-18.3.4); Kubernetes CNI policy flows (see UC-18.3.1); controller-submitted configuration audit diffs without running software normalization (see UC-18.3.8); passive NetFlow-only estates with no inventory poll of OS trains.
dataSources: `index=dc_fabric` / `index=network_ops` / `index=cisco_dc` / `index=os` (rename per governance) combining **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:firmware`** (APIC REST collectors mapping **`firmwareCtrlrFwStatusCont`**, **`firmwareARunning`**, **`maintMaintP`** maintenance-policy objects into JSON); **`sourcetype=cisco:nxos:version`** from NX-OS **`show version`** or telemetry (9.x / 10.x trains) aligned with **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** context; **`sourcetype=arista:eos:version`** from EOS **`eAPI`** **`/command-api`** **`show version`** JSON (EOS 4.x families); **`sourcetype=juniper:junos:software`** from NETCONF **`/system/software/runtime`** Yang snapshots (Junos 22.x / 23.x); optional **`sourcetype=sonic:show:version`** (REST **`/api/v1/sonic/show/version`**, 202311+ baselines), **`sourcetype=cumulus:netq:inventory`** (NetQ **`/api/v3/netq/inventory`**, Cumulus Linux 5.x); gNMI OpenConfig **`/openconfig-system:system/state/software-version`** streams for NOS that export streaming version counters; supplemental **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** for ISSU / non-ISSU reload cues when version polls lag.
app: **Cisco ACI Splunk Integration** ([Splunkbase 4022](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) for APIC firmware MO ingestion into **`cisco:aci:firmware`**; **Splunk Add-on for Cisco Nexus** (**Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus**, Splunkbase **6876**) for **`cisco:nxos:version`** / **`cisco:nxos:syslog`**; **Splunk Add-on for Arista Networks** (**Splunk_TA_arista**, Splunkbase **4291**) for **`arista:eos:version`**; **Splunk Add-on for Juniper** (**Splunk_TA_junos**, Splunkbase **2847**) for **`juniper:junos:software`**; Heavy Forwarder modular inputs or **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase 1546](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for SONiC / Cumulus HTTPS pollers; optional Splunk Add-on for SDN Inventory patterns where deployments standardize **`fabric_id`** keys across vendors.
spl:
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=os)
(sourcetype="cisco:aci:firmware" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:version" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:version" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:software")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval device_name=trim(coalesce(device_name, hostname, switch_name, node_name, host, ""))
| eval vendor=lower(trim(coalesce(vendor, manufacturer, vendor_name, "")))
| eval os_version=trim(coalesce(os_version, running_version, sw_version, nxos_version, eos_version, junos_version, firmware_version, image_version, ""))
| eval fabric_id=trim(coalesce(fabric_id, fabric_name, apic_pod, site_code, ""))
| eval role=lower(trim(coalesce(role, switch_role, device_role, tier, "")))
| eval upgrade_wave_id=trim(coalesce(upgrade_wave_id, wave_id, maint_policy, maintMaintP_dn, ""))
| eval target_version=trim(coalesce(target_version, approved_train, golden_version, wave_target_build, ""))
| eval serial_number=trim(coalesce(serial_number, chassis_serial, ""))
| eval criticality=upper(trim(coalesce(criticality, defect_band, advisory_severity, "")))
| eval last_upgrade_ts=tonumber(coalesce(last_upgrade_ts, last_install_epoch, image_install_time, ""))
| eval last_upgrade_ts=if(isnull(last_upgrade_ts) OR last_upgrade_ts=0, _time, last_upgrade_ts)
| eval eol_date=trim(coalesce(eol_date, eos_sw_support_date, sw_eos_date, ""))
| lookup fabric_wave_calendar upgrade_wave_id OUTPUT wave_start_epoch AS wave_start_ts
| eval wave_start_ts=if(isnull(wave_start_ts), relative_time(now(), "-31d@d"), tonumber(wave_start_ts))
| eval on_target=if(isnotnull(target_version) AND os_version==target_version, 1, 0)
| eval wave_age_days=round((now()-wave_start_ts)/86400, 1)
| eval straggler_flag=if(on_target=0 AND isnotnull(target_version) AND wave_age_days>30, 1, 0)
| eval wave_bucket=case(on_target=1 AND isnotnull(target_version), "Approved", straggler_flag=1, "Stragglers", on_target=0 AND isnotnull(target_version), "Non-Compliant", 1=1, "Review")
| eval eol_epoch=if(match(eol_date,"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}"), strptime(eol_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), null())
| eval days_until_eol=if(isnotnull(eol_epoch), round((eol_epoch-now())/86400, 0), null())
| eval eol_risk=if(isnotnull(days_until_eol) AND days_until_eol>=0 AND days_until_eol<365, 1, 0)
| dedup device_name fabric_id sortby - _time
| eventstats dc(device_name) AS fabric_devices BY fabric_id
| eventstats sum(on_target) AS fabric_on_target BY fabric_id
| eventstats avg(on_target) AS role_hit BY fabric_id role
| eval fabric_compliance_pct=round(100*fabric_on_target/nullif(fabric_devices,0), 2)
| eval role_compliance_pct=round(100*role_hit, 2)
| stats latest(fabric_compliance_pct) AS fabric_compliance_pct latest(role_compliance_pct) AS role_compliance_pct latest(os_version) AS os_latest latest(target_version) AS tgt_latest latest(straggler_flag) AS straggler latest(days_until_eol) AS days_until_eol latest(eol_risk) AS eol_alert latest(role) AS role latest(vendor) AS vendor latest(wave_bucket) AS wave_state latest(criticality) AS crit_band latest(wave_age_days) AS wave_age_days BY fabric_id device_name
| eval severity=case(eol_alert=1 AND straggler=1, "EoL_and_Straggler", eol_alert=1, "EoL_Countdown", straggler=1, "Straggler", wave_state="Non-Compliant", "Non-Compliant", 1=1, "Hygiene")
| sort fabric_id role device_name
| head 400
description: Compares normalized switch network-operating-system and firmware revisions across Cisco NX-OS 9.x and 10.x trains, Cisco APIC controller and linecard images modeled through firmwareCtrlrFwStatusCont and firmwareARunning alongside maintMaintP maintenance-policy wave tags, Arista EOS 4.x trains parsed from show version JSON, Juniper Junos 22.x and 23.x runtime tuples, SONiC 202311 or newer builds, and Cumulus Linux 5.x NetQ inventory rows—then measures each fabric against approved upgrade-wave targets, ISSU versus non-ISSU constraints, vendor end-of-life and end-of-sale clocks mirrored from Cisco EOX APIs, Arista EOS Lifecycle Notice feeds, Juniper end-of-life portal extracts mapped into lookups, Critical High Medium advisory buckets across PSIRT programs, SDN Inventory add-on or vendor TAs where adopted, optional gNMI software-version exports, and per-role leaf spine edge posture so Stragglers more than thirty days behind an approved wave after realized dates, Non-Compliant mixes, In-Progress maintenance waves, and end-of-life countdown windows surface before defects widen.
value: Drift across NX-OS, EOS, Junos, SONiC, and Cumulus trains fractures interoperability, ISSU eligibility, and PSIRT remediation SLAs—one Splunk posture blends fabric compliance percentage, wave realization, EoL proximity, serial-attributed lifecycle facts, and role-based deviations into remediation queues before outages or audit exceptions accumulate.
implementation: Land one sourcetype union per fabric comparing compliance_pct to fabric_wave_calendar.csv targets before wiring Cisco EOX OAuth—that proves ingest parity across Arista, Juniper, and Cisco collectors. Normalize sourcetype families, FIELDALIAS device_name and os_version, refresh nightly lookups from wave planners plus optional EOX serial pulls, and schedule fabric_upgrade_compliance_core saved searches every fifteen or sixty minutes.
visualization: (1) Single-value tiles per **`fabric_id`** — **`fabric_compliance_pct`** with threshold coloring (green ≥95%). (2) Table — **`device_name`**, **`vendor`**, **`os_latest`**, **`tgt_latest`**, **`wave_state`**, **`straggler`**, **`days_until_eol`**, **`severity`**. (3) Column chart — **`wave_bucket`** counts stacked by **`fabric_id`**. (4) Heat matrix — **`role`** (leaf / spine / edge) × **`vendor`** showing average **`fabric_compliance_pct`**. (5) Timechart — trailing thirty-day **`fabric_compliance_pct`** trend lines per **`fabric_id`** overlaying **`wave_start_ts`** markers. (6) Optional bar chart — **`days_until_eol`** buckets (<90, 90–180, 180–365 days).
schema: CIM: Inventory (device identity, serial_number, fabric and role attributes, support_status mirrors) plus Updates (package and patch identifiers mapped from os_version and target_version, severity tiering aligned to advisory criticality where populated).
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Inventory once stable **`device_name`** / **`serial_number`** keys exist via **tstats**; Updates overlays when FIELDALIAS maps **`os_version`** into package semantics—retain parallel raw indexes because APIC MO naming differs from EOS JSON paths.

references.title: Cisco EOX API — support tools REST overview
Arista EOS Lifecycle Notice — documentation hub
Juniper Networks End-of-Life Product Notice portal

knownFalsePositives:
**Certification lab beta trains** — validation switches intentionally run not-yet-approved builds stamped beta or cert inside CMDB environment=lab; Splunk surfaces Non-Compliant rows though networking labs expect divergence—suppress using lab_fabric tokens plus CMDB validation=true markers referencing certification bridges tied to vendor QA workflows.

**Pre-production staging fabrics** — engineers deliberately pin N-minus-one or N-minus-two builds outside production wave calendars until cutover weekends execute—tie suppression macros to staging snow CHG families instead of paging raw mismatches alone during rehearsal weekends or load-sim weekends that never touch customer traffic paths.

**Cold spare sleds** — warehouse shelves remain pinned on older trains until rack insertion tickets attach fabric_id markers—exclude inventory_state spare rows from Sev-3 paging queues until assets attach to production fabrics so procurement staging shelves stop ringing ops bridges nightly.

**Quarantined RMA hold** — devices remain on end-of-support software while awaiting field replace shipments documented inside risk acceptance memos—join quarantine lookups plus deferral CHG identifiers before escalating lifecycle breaches attributed strictly to firmware drift when logistics—not policy—controls the timeline.

**Matched-version DR pairs** — disaster-recovery fabrics deliberately mirror paired builds across metro rings although calendar waves differ—suppress drift via dr_site parity annotations referencing matched_pair_wave planners rather than labeling rogue posture during deliberate symmetry drills or multi-hour failback rehearsals.
hardware: Nexus 9332C, Arista DCS-7280, Juniper QFX5120, Edgecore SONiC DX010 — representative leaf or spine platforms spanning merchant silicon programs referenced in upgrade-wave matrices; serial-level EOX joins follow chassis serial fields extracted from show inventory, APIC DME, or Arista eAPI payloads according to platform SME baselines.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites** — Pin Splunkbase releases 4022 (ACI), 6876 (Nexus), 4291 (Arista), 2847 (Juniper) on Heavy Forwarders sized for HTTPS bursts toward **APIC** clusters and Arista eAPI endpoints. Allocate least-privilege service identities per vendor API plane; rotate OAuth secrets using Splunk credential stores rather than plaintext tokens beside modular inputs. Reserve index dc_fabric or governance-approved equivalents with tiered retention schedules mapped to quarterly audit attestations. Document poll intervals per collector family—**APIC** firmware pulls rarely exceed fifteen-minute cadences without stressing controllers—while scripted **NX-OS** snapshots align with nightly maintenance bridges unless **ISSU** rehearsals demand tighter loops.

**Step 1 — Data sources** — Poll Cisco **APIC** REST class endpoints **firmwareCtrlrFwStatusCont.json**, **firmwareARunning.json**, and **maintMaintP.json** via collectors emitting sourcetype **cisco:aci:firmware** with ctrlFwRunning and maintMaintP_dn mirrored into upgrade_wave_id once TA mappings stabilize. Ship **NX-OS** scripted show version snapshots or telemetry adapters emitting sourcetype **cisco:nxos:version** and correlate sourcetype **cisco:nxos:syslog** whenever **ISSU** versus **non-ISSU** reload wording clarifies polls lagging reality. Pull NVIDIA NetQ HTTPS /api/v3/netq/inventory payloads into **cumulus:netq:inventory** when **Cumulus Linux** 5.x appears in spine tiers. Configure Arista eAPI command-api POST bodies requesting show version JSON for **arista:eos:version**. Subscribe **Junos** NETCONF primitives hitting junos-system software runtime branches feeding **juniper:junos:software**. Issue SONiC REST /api/v1/sonic/show/version requests mapped to **sonic:show:version** when builds stay on 202311 or newer trains. Subscribe gNMI OpenConfig software-version paths where merchant silicon exposes streaming leases without SSH scraping. Hydrate nightly lifecycle bridges using Cisco **EOX** REST https://api.cisco.com/supporttools/eox/rest/5/EOXBySerialNumber/1/{serial}, Arista **EOS** Lifecycle Notice extracts, Juniper end-of-life CSV snapshots, and procurement-managed serial maps so **eol_date** and **eos_date** arrive beside **serial_number**. When **Splunk Add-on for SDN Inventory** already standardizes naming, reuse those transforms rather than fork competing **FIELDALIAS** stacks across teams.

**Step 2 — Field extractions** — Encode **props.conf** and **transforms.conf** pairs extracting **device_name**, vendor, **os_train** covering **NX-OS**, **EOS**, **Junos**, SONiC, Cumulus flavors, plus **os_version**, target_version, upgrade_wave_id, last_upgrade_ts, **eol_date**, **eos_date**, isuu_capable, **fabric_id**, role enumerating leaf spine edge placements, **serial_number**, support_status spanning active through end-of-vulnerability-support stages, and criticality tiers Critical High Medium. Preserve os_version_raw alongside os_version_compare macros stripping marketing fluff before equality checks feeding **PSIRT** lookups. Fold **ISSU** indicators from structured telemetry plus syslog **ISSU** banners so **non-ISSU** reload policies avoid mistaken approvals. Align **Inventory** vendor strings with procurement catalogs—avoid mismatched capitalization between **EOS** versus eos—that otherwise fracture **dedup** joins across fabrics sharing naming dictionaries only loosely coupled.

**Step 3 — Initial validation** — Compare Splunk latest **os_version** summaries against Cisco-only Image Management widgets inside **NDFC** strictly as fidelity checks—not governance truth for multi-vendor estates. Mirror Arista **CloudVision Software Management**, Juniper Apstra image trackers, SONiC ONL manifests solely for ingest parity versus consoles while CAB-approved **fabric_wave_calendar.csv** remains authoritative. Reconcile **EOX**-derived **eol_date** rows against Arista portal snapshots and Juniper notice exports quarterly with procurement escalation beyond seven-day deltas. Audit **serial_number** joins randomly across five percent of each upgrade_wave_id cohort referencing **APIC** distinguished names or **EOS** inventory APIs prior to paging stakeholders. Capture screenshots or REST excerpts inside change tickets whenever validation discrepancies emerge so auditors inherit reproducible breadcrumbs beyond Splunk rows alone.

**Step 4 — SPL building blocks** — Compute **fabric_compliance_pct** per **fabric_id** using **dedup** snapshots followed by sum(on_target) divided by dc(**device_name**) so telemetry bursts during **ISSU** maintenance windows avoid duplicate chassis counts. Raise straggler_flag after wave_age_days exceeds thirty days while target_version persists yet **os_version** mismatches—the backlog indicator beyond CAB realization timelines. Emit **eol_risk** whenever **days_until_eol** stays nonnegative yet beneath three hundred sixty-five days while expired negatives route separately via procurement exemptions. Trend **wave_bucket** mixes via **timechart** overlays keyed by **fabric_id** illustrating **Approved** versus **Stragglers** versus **Non-Compliant** populations. Produce **role_compliance_pct** via **eventstats** avg(on_target) partitioned by **fabric_id** and role highlighting spine divergence independently from leaf churn. Supply vendor facets contrasting **EOS** uptake versus **NX-OS** uptake inside identical fabrics exposing procurement divergence sooner than spreadsheets recalculate duplicates. Maintain macros **fabric_inv_version_base** wrapping earliest clauses so dashboards inherit governance-approved index unions without exposing raw prod-plus-lab collisions inadvertently.

**Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns** — Correlate Cisco **PSIRT** **openVuln** advisories through lookups keyed by vendor, **os_train** major.minor tuples, criticality tiers, and **os_version** tuples so **Splunk Enterprise Security** risk modifiers escalate Critical overlaps whenever remediation slips beyond wave planners. Subscribe vendor field-notice CSV bridges enriching **serial_number** rows with thermal FPGA notices adjunct to upgrades yet unrelated logically. Blend **cmdb_ci** lookups stamping tier atop **device_name** prioritizing Sev-2 escalations toward revenue-bearing racks before lab benches. Join ServiceNow CHG extracts referencing upgrade_wave_id tokens embedded inside short_description narratives comparing last_upgrade_ts versus planned_end timestamps for forensic mismatches. Attach **fabric_precheck** JSON payloads noting optics BER faults suppressing alerts where upgrades aborted despite compliant manifests owing to unrelated hardware faults. Layer **post_upgrade_soak** macros muting BGP syslog churn forty-eight to seventy-two hours after last_upgrade_ts whenever synthetic probes remain green though protocols remain noisy. Fold MITRE ATTACK technique **T1542.005** firmware-modification hunts aligning Critical overlaps with **Updates** CIM semantics so auditors consume coherent evidence packs. Orchestrate Splunk ES adaptive responses referencing risk_score deltas whenever cve_match intersects production tiers documented inside asset_priority lookups. Pair **Splunk ITSI** maintenance windows anchored on **fabric_id** services suppressing paging noise yet informing predictive scheduling ahead of CAB freezes via episode_density KPI slopes describing upgrade-heavy episodes. Feed optional SOAR playbooks referencing change_request_id bridges so closed-loop remediation reopens only when soak timers expire without renewed compliance positives.

**Step 6 — Operational runbook** — Trigger Sev-3 paging when wave_state stays **Non-Compliant** across two successive fifteen-minute cadences lacking staging CMDB markers—ticket templates cite **fabric_compliance_pct**, wave_age_days, **role_compliance_pct**, tgt_latest anchors so responders inherit quantitative facts; cite vendor-published **ISSU** MTBF guidance alongside operational MTTR medians mined from historical ServiceNow closures filtered per **fabric_id**. Escalate Sev-2 when **days_until_eol** dips below one hundred eighty days on tier-one workloads referencing archived **EOX** snapshot identifiers inserted into incident bridges. Publish **Dashboard Studio** tokens **fabric_id** spanning single-value tiles for **fabric_compliance_pct**, tabular Straggler drilldowns, matrices plotting **days_until_eol** buckets, wave-progress timecharts, stacked vendor comparisons, and role heat ladders. Convene weekly CAB scrubs reconciling Straggler backlog versus wave_calendar ownership reopening dormant CHG bridges whenever wave_age_days exceeds forty-five days without remediation commentary. Rotate OAuth credentials guarding **EOX** and **openVuln** collectors quarterly attaching vault ticket identifiers inside SOAR evidence repositories preserving audit traceability. Reinforce escalation etiquette: Sev-3 remains workflow debt, Sev-2 remains risk-to-life for EoL proximity, Sev-1 reserved for Critical **PSIRT** overlaps breaching interim mitigations documented in incident runbooks referencing this fabric inventory feed.

**Governance annex** — Maintain architecture decision records stating why multi-vendor normalization beats single-pane **NDFC**-only dashboards for your estate; archive Splunk saved-search revisions weekly when **FIELDALIAS** transforms shift during TA upgrades so auditors correlate alert firing changes with vendor parser cadence rather than blaming unexplained drift. Document credential custodians alongside OAuth client identifiers stored inside Splunk secret stores—never embed operator LDAP passwords inside modular inputs merely because expedient testing demanded shortcuts during outage bridges. Extend lookup retention for **fabric_wave_calendar.csv** and serial_to_eol.csv inside Git-backed repositories reviewed through pull requests mirroring CAB approvals so Splunk lookup drift inherits the same authorization trail as router CLI changes executed manually by senior engineers during freeze windows.
grandmaExplanation: We line up each switch's software version against the rollout wave your engineers scheduled—even when vendors differ—and call attention when something stays behind too long or nears the date vendors stop fixing problems. That heads off breakage from mismatched trains and closes patching gaps earlier.
